title: First record of Daphnia retrocurva Forbes, 1882 (Crustacea, Cladocera, Daphniidae) outside its native North America (Shin Toyone Reservoir, Japan)
author: Makino, Wataru
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Daphnia retrocurva  Forbes, 1882 (Crustacea, Cladocera, Daphniidae), a North American freshwater zooplankton species, was found in an artificial reservoir in central Japan. This represents the first record of  D. retrocurva  not only in Japanese and Asian freshwaters but also from outside North America. Our phylogenetic analysis based on mitochondrial DNA sequences revealed little differentiation between Japanese and North American populations. By combining geographical isolation and the lack of genetic differentiation, we consider Japanese  D. retrocurva  to be non-indigenous and relatively recently introduced.
steps_raw: Daphnia retrocurva  Forbes, 1882 (Crustacea, Cladocera, Daphniidae), a North American freshwater zooplankton species, was found in an artificial reservoir in central Japan. Our phylogenetic analysis based on mitochondrial DNA sequences revealed little differentiation between Japanese and North American populations. By combining geographical isolation and the lack of genetic differentiation, we consider Japanese  D. retrocurva  to be non-indigenous and relatively recently introduced.
